title: Cross-Border Transaction Reporting Requirements
content:
MEMORANDUM TO: Senior Advisors and Above
FROM: Compliance Department
DATE: March 15, 2025
RE: Updated Cross-Border Transaction Reporting Requirements

This memorandum outlines critical updates to cross-border transaction reporting requirements affecting Meridian Wealth Partners' international client portfolios and foreign securities transactions. All senior-level personnel must ensure strict adherence to these enhanced protocols.

REGULATORY FRAMEWORK

Under SEC Rule 15c3-5 (Market Access Rule), all cross-border transactions exceeding $250,000 in aggregate value within a 30-day period require pre-trade risk controls and enhanced documentation. Additionally, FINRA Rule 6830 mandates comprehensive reporting for foreign exchange transactions and international wire transfers that constitute part of securities transactions.

REPORTING THRESHOLDS AND TIMELINES

Effective immediately, the following transactions require mandatory reporting within 24 hours of execution:

• Foreign equity purchases/sales exceeding $500,000 per client account
• Currency hedging transactions above $1,000,000 notional value
• International bond transactions involving non-US sovereign debt exceeding $750,000
• Structured products with cross-border underlying assets above $300,000

For transactions between $100,000 and the above thresholds, standard T+2 reporting applies through our internal COMPASS system.

CLIENT CLASSIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

All clients with international exposure must be classified according to their cross-border activity level:

Tier A: >$5M in foreign assets (quarterly comprehensive reviews)
Tier B: $1M-$5M in foreign assets (semi-annual reviews)
Tier C: <$1M in foreign assets (annual reviews)

DOCUMENTATION STANDARDS

Each cross-border transaction file must contain:

1. Completed Form MW-4B (International Transaction Authorization)
2. Currency risk disclosure acknowledgment
3. Tax reporting implications summary
4. Beneficial ownership certification for accounts >$2M
5. Anti-money laundering verification (updated within 12 months)

SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS

Particular attention must be paid to transactions involving:

• Emerging market securities (additional 0.25% compliance fee applies)
• Private placements with foreign issuers (48-hour pre-approval required)
• Cryptocurrency-related foreign investments (currently restricted pending regulatory clarity)
• Real estate investment trusts domiciled outside North America

TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION

Our updated MERIDIAN-TRACK system now automatically flags transactions requiring cross-border reporting. The system integrates with our existing risk management platform and provides real-time compliance scoring based on transaction complexity and regulatory requirements.

PENALTIES AND ENFORCEMENT

Recent SEC enforcement actions have resulted in fines averaging $2.3M for inadequate cross-border reporting. FINRA has imposed sanctions ranging from $50,000 to $500,000 for firms failing to maintain proper documentation standards.

ACTION ITEMS

All senior advisors must:

1. Complete updated cross-border compliance training by March 31, 2025
2. Review existing client portfolios for proper classification
3. Update client communication templates to reflect new disclosure requirements
4. Coordinate with Operations team for system access updates

Questions regarding specific client situations should be directed to the Compliance Department extension 4421. The International Trading Desk (extension 4445) can provide guidance on execution-related compliance matters.

This memo supersedes all previous cross-border reporting guidance. Failure to comply with these requirements may result in disciplinary action and regulatory exposure for the firm.
